```py cloud/cloud/products/views.py
from django.contrib import messages
from django.db.models import Count, Sum
from django.shortcuts import redirect
from django.views import View
from django.views.generic import DetailView
from django.views.generic.detail import SingleObjectMixin

from cloud.base_views import OtovoListView
from cloud.permissions import OtovoPermissionRequiredMixin, StaffRequiredMixin
from cloud.products.filters import ProductFilter
from cloud.products.models import CustomerProduct, Product


class CustomerProductDetailView(StaffRequiredMixin, DetailView):
    context_object_name = 'product'
    model = CustomerProduct

    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
        context = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
        obj = self.get_object()
        context['title'] = f'Customer product: {obj}'
        return context


class CustomerProductCancel(OtovoPermissionRequiredMixin, SingleObjectMixin, View):
    model = CustomerProduct
    permission_required = 'products.customerproduct_cancel'

    def post(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        product = self.get_object()

        if not product.canceled_at:
            try:
                product.cancel()
                messages.success(request, f'Canceled {product}.')
            except ValueError as exc:
                messages.error(request, str(exc))

        else:
            messages.error(request, f'{product} is already canceled.')
        next_url = self.request.GET.get('next')
        if next_url:
            return redirect(next_url)

        return redirect(product)


class ProductListView(StaffRequiredMixin, OtovoListView):
    context_object_name = 'products'
    model = Product
    title = 'Products'
    filter_class = ProductFilter
    default_order = '-product_number'

    def get_base_queryset(self):
        return Product.objects.annotate(
            num_customer_products=Count('customerproduct', distinct=True),
            num_invoice_items=Count('customerproduct__invoiceitem', distinct=True),
            amount_invoiced_no_vat=Sum('customerproduct__invoiceitem__total_price_no_vat'),
            amount_invoiced_with_vat=Sum('customerproduct__invoiceitem__total_price_with_vat'),
        ).all()
```

```py cloud/cloud/products/filters.py
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypeAlias

from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

import django_filters

from cloud.location.enums import Country
from cloud.products.enums import ProductCategory, PurchaseModel
from cloud.products.models import Product

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from django.db.models import QuerySet

    Q_PRODUCT: TypeAlias = QuerySet[Product]


BOOL_CHOICES = [(True, _('Yes')), (False, _('No'))]


class ProductFilter(django_filters.FilterSet):
    """
    Django filter which filters products.
    """

    query = django_filters.CharFilter(label='Search by name', method='search')
    country = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(choices=Country.choices(), field_name='country', label='Country')
    product_category = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(
        choices=ProductCategory.choices(), field_name='product_category', label='Product Category'
    )
    purchase_model = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(
        choices=PurchaseModel.valid_choices(), field_name='purchase_model', label='Purchase Model'
    )
    external_invoicing = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(
        choices=BOOL_CHOICES, field_name='external_invoicing', label='External Invoicing'
    )
    external_settlement = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(
        choices=BOOL_CHOICES, field_name='external_settlement', label='External Settlement'
    )
    ordering = django_filters.OrderingFilter(
        fields=[
            'created_at',
            'country',
            'purchase_model',
            'product_category',
            'num_customer_products',
            'num_invoice_items',
        ],
    )

    class Meta:
        model = Product
        fields = ['query', 'ordering']

    @staticmethod
    def search(queryset: Q_PRODUCT, _, value: str) -> Q_PRODUCT:
        if not value:
            return queryset
        return queryset.filter(name__icontains=value)
```


I need to update the search to either search by name, like it does today, or `product.product_number`.
To update the search functionality to allow searching by either `name` or `product_number`, you can modify the `search` method in the `ProductFilter` class to include an additional filter for `product_number`. Here's the revised code for `ProductFilter`:

```py
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypeAlias

from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

import django_filters

from cloud.location.enums import Country
from cloud.products.enums import ProductCategory, PurchaseModel
from cloud.products.models import Product

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from django.db.models import QuerySet

    Q_PRODUCT: TypeAlias = QuerySet[Product]


BOOL_CHOICES = [(True, _('Yes')), (False, _('No'))]


class ProductFilter(django_filters.FilterSet):
    """
    Django filter which filters products.
    """

    query = django_filters.CharFilter(label='Search by name or product number', method='search')
    country = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(choices=Country.choices(), field_name='country', label='Country')
    product_category = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(
        choices=ProductCategory.choices(), field_name='product_category', label='Product Category'
    )
    purchase_model = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(
        choices=PurchaseModel.valid_choices(), field_name='purchase_model', label='Purchase Model'
    )
    external_invoicing = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(
        choices=BOOL_CHOICES, field_name='external_invoicing', label='External Invoicing'
    )
    external_settlement = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(
        choices=BOOL_CHOICES, field_name='external_settlement', label='External Settlement'
    )
    ordering = django_filters.OrderingFilter(
        fields=[
            'created_at',
            'country',
            'purchase_model',
            'product_category',
            'num_customer_products',
            'num_invoice_items',
        ],
    )

    class Meta:
        model = Product
        fields = ['query', 'ordering']

    @staticmethod
    def search(queryset: Q_PRODUCT, _, value: str) -> Q_PRODUCT:
        if not value:
            return queryset
        return queryset.filter(
            Q(name__icontains=value) |
            Q(product_number__icontains=value)
        )
```

Make sure to include the necessary import for `Q` from `django.db.models`:

```py
from django.db.models import Q
```

Now the `search` method is updated to filter the queryset by either `name` or `product_number` based on the user's input in the `query` field. This should satisfy the requirement to search by name or product number.
Worked perfectly, thanks! 
You're welcome! I'm glad to hear that it worked perfectly. If you have any more questions or need further assistance, feel free to ask. Happy coding!
```py cloud/cloud/financial/salesorder/filter.py
from django.db.models import Q
from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

import django_filters

from cloud.businessunits.mixins import CountryFilterMixin
from cloud.customers.filters import get_customer_filter
from cloud.financial.salesorder.enums import Status
from cloud.financial.salesorder.models import Order


class SalesOrderFilter(CountryFilterMixin, django_filters.FilterSet):
    query = django_filters.CharFilter(label='Search query', method='search')
    status = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(choices=Status.choices, label=_('Status'))
    ordering = django_filters.OrderingFilter(fields=['id', 'customer', 'project'])

    class Meta:
        model = Order
        fields = [
            'query',
            'status',
            'country',
        ]

    def search(self, queryset, name, value):
        return queryset.filter(
            Q(project__project_number__iexact=value) | get_customer_filter(value, prefix='customer__')
        )
```

```py cloud/cloud/financial/salesorder/views.py
import logging
from json import JSONDecodeError
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
from uuid import UUID

from django.conf import settings
from django.contrib import messages
from django.db import transaction
from django.db.models import Count, Prefetch
from django.http import HttpResponse
from django.shortcuts import get_object_or_404, redirect, render
from django.urls import reverse
from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _
from django.views.generic import View
from django.views.generic.detail import DetailView, SingleObjectMixin
from django.views.generic.edit import FormView

from cloud.base_views import OtovoListView
from cloud.businessunits.mixins import StaffObjectMixin
from cloud.contracts.models.contract import Contract
from cloud.contracts.models.direct_debit_agreement import DirectDebitAgreement
from cloud.financial.payments_module.endpoints.service import PaymentsService
from cloud.financial.payments_module.exceptions.payments import PaymentsException
from cloud.financial.salesorder.danger_zone import get_invoicing_customer
from cloud.financial.salesorder.enums import BillingSchedule, Status
from cloud.financial.salesorder.filter import SalesOrderFilter
from cloud.financial.salesorder.forms import SalesOrderCommentForm
from cloud.financial.salesorder.models import Order
from cloud.financial.salesorder.tasks import process_sales_order
from cloud.location.enums import Country
from cloud.permissions import StaffRequiredMixin, staff_required
from cloud.products.enums import PurchaseModel

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _process_order(order, request):
    if order.status not in [Status.QUEUED, Status.OPEN, Status.FAILED]:
        msg = 'This order is not ready for processing'
        messages.error(request, msg)
        return redirect(order)
    if order.status == Status.FAILED:
        order_project = order.project
        if order_project.is_abandoned and order_project.purchase_model != PurchaseModel.BUY_NOW:
            logger.info('Trying to change purchase model of order: %s', order.id)
            order_customer = order.customer
            PaymentsService.change_purchase_model(order_project, PurchaseModel.BUY_NOW)
            if order_customer != order.project.customer:
                logger.info('Customer is wrong for this purchase model so updating customer')
                order.customer = order.project.customer
                order.save(update_fields=['customer'])
    job_id = process_sales_order.delay(order.id)
    logger.info('Received celery job ID %s', job_id)
    if order.status in [Status.QUEUED, Status.FAILED]:
        order.status = Status.PROCESSING
        order.save(update_fields=['status'])
    return redirect(order)


class SalesOrderListView(StaffRequiredMixin, OtovoListView):
    model = Order
    title = 'Sales Orders'
    filter_class = SalesOrderFilter
    template_name = 'salesorder/order_list.html'

    def get_base_queryset(self):
        qs = (
            self.model.all_for_staff_user(self.request.user)
            .select_related(
                'project',
                'customer',
                'project__address__zip_code',
            )
            .prefetch_related(
                Prefetch(
                    'project__contract_set',
                    queryset=Contract.project_objects.all(),
                    to_attr='active_contracts',
                ),
            )
            .annotate(num_items=Count('item'))
        )
        return qs

    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
        context = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
        context['countries'] = Country.valid_choices()
        return context


class SalesOrderDetailView(StaffRequiredMixin, StaffObjectMixin, DetailView, FormView):
    context_object_name = 'order'
    model = Order
    title = 'Sales order detail'
    template_name = 'salesorder/order_detail.html'
    form_class = SalesOrderCommentForm

    if TYPE_CHECKING:

        def get_object(self, queryset=None) -> Order: ...

    def get_queryset(self):
        return (
            super()
            .get_queryset()
            .select_related('project', 'customer', 'business_unit')
            .prefetch_related('item_set', 'events')
            .annotate(num_items=Count('item'))
        )

    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
        context = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
        order = self.get_object()
        context['invoicing_customer'] = get_invoicing_customer(order.project)
        context['comment_form'] = SalesOrderCommentForm(self.request.POST)
        context['events'] = order.events.all()
        context['items'] = order.item_set.all().select_related('product')
        context['payments_url'] = settings.PAYMENTS_API_EXTERNAL_URL
        context['status_change_disabled'] = order.status not in Status.valid_values_for_status_change()
        return context

    def get_success_url(self) -> str:
        return self.get_object().get_absolute_url()

    def form_valid(self, form):
        self.get_object().change_property(
            field=Order.status, new_value=form.cleaned_data['status'], user=self.request.user
        )
        return super().form_valid(form)


class ProcessSalesOrder(StaffRequiredMixin, StaffObjectMixin, SingleObjectMixin, View):
    model = Order

    @transaction.atomic
    def post(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        order = self.get_object()
        return _process_order(order, request)


class CancelSalesOrder(StaffRequiredMixin, StaffObjectMixin, SingleObjectMixin, View):
    model = Order

    @transaction.atomic
    def post(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        order: Order = self.get_object()
        if order.billing_schedule != BillingSchedule.DIRECT_PURCHASE:
            msg = """Only direct purchase orders can be canceled this way,
            use cancel subscription button on the project page if thats what you want to do."""
            logger.info(msg)
            messages.error(self.request, msg)
            return redirect(order)
        try:
            msg = 'Canceling direct purchase order %s', str(order.id)
            logger.info(msg)
            PaymentsService.cancel_direct_purchase(order.id)
        except (PaymentsException, JSONDecodeError) as e:
            messages.error(self.request, str(e))
            return redirect(order)
        order.status = Status.CANCELLED
        order.save()
        return redirect(order)


class SubmitBic(StaffRequiredMixin, StaffObjectMixin, SingleObjectMixin, View):
    model = Order

    @transaction.atomic
    def post(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        order: Order = self.get_object()
        bic_value = request.POST['bic_value']
        assert len(bic_value) in [8, 11], 'BIC must be 8 or 11 characters long'
        assert bic_value.isalnum(), 'BIC must be alphanumeric'
        messages.info(self.request, f"Set bic on the project to {request.POST['bic_value']}")

        dd_agreement = (
            DirectDebitAgreement.objects.exclude(iban__isnull=True, bic__isnull=True, bic='', iban='')
            .filter(contract__project=order.project)
            .order_by('-created_at')
            .first()
        )
        assert dd_agreement, 'No Direct Debit Agreement'
        dd_agreement.bic = bic_value
        dd_agreement.save()
        order.project.events.create(
            name='manualy_set_bic',
            payload={'internal': True},
            created_by=request.user,
        )
        return _process_order(order, request)


@staff_required
@transaction.atomic
def toggle_status_change_hx(request, pk: UUID) -> HttpResponse:
    sales_order = get_object_or_404(Order, pk=pk)
    show_select = request.GET.get('show_select') == 'true'
    status_change_disabled = sales_order.status not in Status.valid_values_for_status_change()

    if request.method == 'POST':
        new_status = request.POST.get('status_change')

        if status_change_disabled:
            messages.error(request, _('Status change is disabled for this order'))
        elif new_status not in Status.valid_values_for_status_change():
            messages.error(request, _('Cannot change to status {status}').format(status=new_status))
        elif new_status == sales_order.status:
            messages.success(request, _('Saved'))
        else:
            sales_order.change_property(field=Order.status, new_value=new_status, user=request.user)
            messages.success(request, _('Changed status to {status}').format(status=new_status))

        # Redirect to force a reload of the page
        return HttpResponse(
            headers={'HX-Redirect': reverse('payments_module:salesorder:detail', args=[sales_order.pk])}
        )

    context = {
        'show_status_select': show_select,
        'initial_load': False,
        'choices': Status.valid_choices_for_status_change(),
        'initial': sales_order.status,
        'order': sales_order,
    }

    return render(request, 'salesorder/order_status_row_hx.html', context)
```


Similar to earlier, I want to add an BooleanFilter to the sales order listview to filter sales orders (Order class) that have `shopping_cart` FK.
To add a `BooleanFilter` to the `SalesOrderListView` for filtering the sales orders based on whether they have a related `shopping_cart`, you need to make the following changes:

1. Update the `SalesOrderFilter` class to include the new `BooleanFilter`.
2. Update the templates to include the new filter option if needed.

Here's the modified `SalesOrderFilter` class with the added `BooleanFilter`:

### Updated `SalesOrderFilter`:

```py
from django.db.models import Q, Exists, OuterRef
from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

import django_filters

from cloud.businessunits.mixins import CountryFilterMixin
from cloud.customers.filters import get_customer_filter
from cloud.financial.salesorder.enums import Status
from cloud.financial.salesorder.models import Order, ShoppingCart

class SalesOrderFilter(CountryFilterMixin, django_filters.FilterSet):
    query = django_filters.CharFilter(label='Search query', method='search')
    status = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(choices=Status.choices, label=_('Status'))
    ordering = django_filters.OrderingFilter(fields=['id', 'customer', 'project'])
    has_shopping_cart = django_filters.BooleanFilter(label='Has Shopping Cart', method='filter_shopping_cart')

    class Meta:
        model = Order
        fields = [
            'query',
            'status',
            'country',
            'has_shopping_cart',
        ]

    def search(self, queryset, name, value):
        return queryset.filter(
            Q(project__project_number__iexact=value) | get_customer_filter(value, prefix='customer__')
        )

    def filter_shopping_cart(self, queryset, _, value):
        if value:
            return queryset.filter(Exists(ShoppingCart.objects.filter(order=OuterRef('pk'))))
        else:
            return queryset.exclude(Exists(ShoppingCart.objects.filter(order=OuterRef('pk'))))
```

In this updated `SalesOrderFilter` class, the `has_shopping_cart` filter is a Boolean filter that will filter the orders based on whether they have an associated `shopping_cart` ForeignKey. The `filter_shopping_cart` method uses `Exists` and `OuterRef` to perform a subquery that checks for the presence of related `ShoppingCart` objects.

### Updating Template to include the new Filter Option:

Make sure to update your template to include the `has_shopping_cart` filter. Typically, this would involve adding a form element for the new filter field. Here's an example on how to update your template (`salesorder/order_list.html`):

```html
<form method="get">
    <!-- Include your other filter fields here -->
    <label for="id_has_shopping_cart">Has Shopping Cart:</label>
    <input type="checkbox" id="id_has_shopping_cart" name="has_shopping_cart" {% if request.GET.has_shopping_cart %}checked{% endif %}>
    <input type="submit" value="Filter">
</form>
```

With these changes, you'll be able to filter sales orders based on whether they have a related `shopping_cart` in the `SalesOrderListView`. Make sure to replace the template example with your specific implementation as needed.

```py cloud/cloud/financial/salesorder/views.py
@transaction.atomic
def toggle_status_change_hx(request, pk: UUID) -> HttpResponse:
    sales_order = get_object_or_404(Order, pk=pk)
    show_select = request.GET.get('show_select') == 'true'
    status_change_disabled = sales_order.status not in Status.valid_values_for_status_change()

    if request.method == 'POST':
        new_status = request.POST.get('status_change')

        if status_change_disabled:
            messages.error(request, _('Status change is disabled for this order'))
        elif new_status not in Status.valid_values_for_status_change():
            messages.error(request, _('Cannot change to status {status}').format(status=new_status))
        elif new_status == sales_order.status:
            messages.success(request, _('Saved'))
        else:
            sales_order.change_property(field=Order.status, new_value=new_status, user=request.user)
            messages.success(request, _('Changed status to {status}').format(status=new_status))

        # Redirect to force a reload of the page
        return HttpResponse(
            headers={'HX-Redirect': reverse('payments_module:salesorder:detail', args=[sales_order.pk])}
        )

    context = {
        'show_status_select': show_select,
        'initial_load': False,
        'choices': Status.valid_choices_for_status_change(),
        'initial': sales_order.status,
        'order': sales_order,
    }

    return render(request, 'salesorder/order_status_row_hx.html', context)
```

```py cloud/cloud/financial/salesorder/filter.py
from django.db.models import Q
from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

import django_filters

from cloud.businessunits.mixins import CountryFilterMixin
from cloud.customers.filters import get_customer_filter
from cloud.financial.salesorder.enums import Status
from cloud.financial.salesorder.models import Order
from cloud.installerapp.filters import BooleanFilterWithEmptyChoice


class SalesOrderFilter(CountryFilterMixin, django_filters.FilterSet):
    query = django_filters.CharFilter(label='Search query', method='search')
    status = django_filters.ChoiceFilter(choices=Status.choices, label=_('Status'))
    ordering = django_filters.OrderingFilter(fields=['id', 'customer', 'project'])
    is_shopping_cart = BooleanFilterWithEmptyChoice(
        label='Is shopping cart',
        field_name='shopping_cart',
        method='filter_shopping_cart',
    )

    class Meta:
        model = Order
        fields = [
            'query',
            'status',
            'country',
        ]

    def search(self, queryset, name, value):
        return queryset.filter(
            Q(project__project_number__iexact=value) | get_customer_filter(value, prefix='customer__')
        )

    def filter_shopping_cart(self, queryset, _, value):
        if value is None:
            return queryset

        return queryset.exclude(shopping_cart__isnull=bool(value))
```


This now works perfectly. Thanks for the help. I want ot add an additional feature to my list view. If we're filtering on `status=failed` I want ot inline the error (i.e. do some conditional logic in the template). Is status already in the context or on the request?
To implement the feature where you conditionally inline the error only when filtering on `status=failed` in your list view, you should handle it in the view and pass the required context to the template.

### Checking if `status` is in the request

You can check if `status` is included in the request parameters and pass that information to the context. Here's how you can modify the `SalesOrderListView` to include this:

### Updating `SalesOrderListView` to include the status in the context:

```py
class SalesOrderListView(StaffRequiredMixin, OtovoListView):
    model = Order
    title = 'Sales Orders'
    filter_class = SalesOrderFilter
    template_name = 'salesorder/order_list.html'

    def get_base_queryset(self):
        qs = (
            self.model.all_for_staff_user(self.request.user)
            .select_related(
                'project',
                'customer',
                'project__address__zip_code',
            )
            .prefetch_related(
                Prefetch(
                    'project__contract_set',
                    queryset=Contract.project_objects.all(),
                    to_attr='active_contracts',
                ),
            )
            .annotate(num_items=Count('item'))
        )
        return qs

    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
        context = super().get_context_data(**kwargs)
        context['countries'] = Country.valid_choices()

        # Check if 'status' is in the request parameters
        status = self.request.GET.get('status', None)
        context['filtered_status'] = status

        return context
```

In this update, the `status` filter parameter (if present) is added to the context as `filtered_status`.

### Updating Template to Use Conditional Logic

Now, you can use this information in your template to conditionally render the error inline when `status=failed`. Here's an example of how you might modify your template (`salesorder/order_list.html`):

```html
<!-- existing filter form and table headers -->
<form method="get">
    <!-- Your other filter fields.. -->
    <label for="id_status">Status:</label>
    <select id="id_status" name="status">
        <option value="">All</option>
        {% for choice_value, choice_label in filter.form.status.field.choices %}
            <option value="{{ choice_value }}" {% if choice_value == request.GET.status %}selected{% endif %}>
                {{ choice_label }}
            </option>
        {% endfor %}
    </select>
    <input type="submit" value="Filter">
</form>

<table>
    <thead>
        <tr>
            <!-- Your table headings -->
            <th>Order ID</th>
            <th>Status</th>
            <th>Customer</th>
            <th>Project</th>
            <!-- Add other headings -->
        </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
        {% for order in salesorders %}
        <tr>
            <td>{{ order.id }}</td>
            <td>{{ order.status }}</td>
            <td>{{ order.customer }}</td>
            <td>{{ order.project }}</td>
            <!-- Add other columns -->
            
            <!-- Conditional logic to inline error -->
            {% if filtered_status == 'failed' and order.status == 'failed' %}
                <td>{{ order.some_error_field }}</td> <!-- Replace with actual error field -->
            {% endif %}
        </tr>
        {% endfor %}
    </tbody>
</table>
```

Here, we've added the `filtered_status` context variable to conditionally display an error inline if the status filter is set to 'failed' and the order's status is also 'failed'. 

Make sure to replace `some_error_field` with the actual field or information that contains the error for the order.

With these changes, your list view will now show inline errors conditionally based on the status filter.
